name: Ralph Moore
abstract: Ralph Moore (born 24 December 1956) is an English jazz saxophonist.

concerts.label: JVC Jazz Festival: Wynton Marsalis Salutes Dizzy Gillespie on His Seventieth Birthday
JVC Jazz Festival: Salute to Benny Goodman
Carnegie Hall Centennial Festival: A Celebration of Jazz: The Carnegie Hall Jazz Band
Carnegie Hall Jazz Band: The Legacy of Miles
Carnegie Hall Jazz Band: The Musical World of Tito Puente

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Barron
about_quotes.evidence: 1990, Invitation – and we’re not even talking about your work as a sideman. These are just you as a leader – and then 1990, Invitation, Ralph Moore, David Williams, and Louis Nash, Criss Cross.
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Barron — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Mike Wofford
about_quotes.evidence: I just was up there earlier this week, I worked with Ray Brown on some recordings up there, with a wonderful — the first time I’d worked with him, I’d admired him on recordings but first time I’d met him or worked with him, Ralph Moore
about_quotes.doc_title: Mike Wofford interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 13, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: James Moody
about_quotes.evidence: I didn't particular care for with the Phillip Morris band... It was nice seeing like Ralph Moore and Dallas Morgan... It was nice being with them.
about_quotes.doc_title: James Moody — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
